auth.safety: Treat API keys like passwords. Do not expose them in browser code, public prompts, logs, or repositories.

globalConventions.ownerScope: All operations are scoped to the authenticated API key owner.
globalConventions.amountFormat: Major currency units with at most 2 decimal places.

fieldSemantics.period: The billing cycle. Send monthly, yearly, or custom. Do not send billingCycle to the v1 API.
fieldSemantics.customDate: The custom interval in days when period is custom. Despite the legacy name, this is not a calendar date. Do not send customIntervalDays to the v1 API.
fieldSemantics.lastPaymentDate: The most recent payment date in YYYY-MM-DD format. The server uses it to calculate nextPaymentDate.

globalConventions.writeConfirmationPolicy: Ask for user confirmation before create, update, or delete. For delete, repeat the target subscription name and id.

tools.name: list_subscriptions
tools.purpose: List subscriptions owned by the API key owner so an agent can search, summarize, compare, or identify records before writing.
useWhen: The user asks to show, search, analyze, or summarize subscriptions.
The user names a subscription but does not provide its UUID.
The agent needs current state before planning an update or delete.
doNotUseWhen: The user provides a known UUID and asks for only that exact record.

tools.name: get_subscription
tools.purpose: Fetch one subscription by UUID when the exact id is already known.
useWhen: The agent needs to verify exact current state before writing.
The user provides a subscription UUID.
doNotUseWhen: The user only provides a name or category. List subscriptions first and match the target.
naturalLanguageExamples: Verify this record before updating it.

id.description: Subscription id returned by list_subscriptions or create_subscription.

tools.name: create_subscription
tools.purpose: Create a new tracked subscription for the API key owner.
tools.confirmationPrompt: I will create a subscription named {name} for {amount} {currency}, billed {period}, with lastPaymentDate {lastPaymentDate}. Confirm?
useWhen: The user asks to add, record, or start tracking a subscription that does not already exist.
doNotUseWhen: The target subscription already exists. Use update_subscription instead.
The user is only asking for analysis or a recommendation.
naturalLanguageExamples: Add ChatGPT Plus, 20 USD monthly, last paid 2026-06-17.
Record a yearly GitHub subscription for 48 USD.

tools.name: update_subscription
tools.purpose: Update one or more writable fields on an existing subscription. The server recalculates nextPaymentDate from lastPaymentDate, period, and customDate.
tools.confirmationPrompt: I will update subscription {id} with these fields: {patch}. Confirm?
useWhen: The target subscription has been identified and the user asks to change amount, category, currency, period, lastPaymentDate, customDate, name, or notificationEnabled.
doNotUseWhen: The user wants to remove the record. Use delete_subscription after explicit confirmation.
The user wants to change nextPaymentDate directly. Update lastPaymentDate and period instead.
naturalLanguageExamples: Turn off notifications for this subscription.

id.description: Subscription id returned by list_subscriptions or create_subscription.

tools.name: delete_subscription
tools.purpose: Permanently delete a tracked subscription record.
tools.confirmationPrompt: I will permanently delete subscription {name} ({id}). Confirm?
useWhen: The user explicitly asks to delete or remove a tracked subscription and confirms the exact target.
doNotUseWhen: The user wants to pause, cancel externally, or mark inactive. The current API only deletes the local tracking record.

id.description: Subscription id returned by list_subscriptions or create_subscription.

error.suggestedFix: Use one of the supported billing periods: monthly, yearly, custom.

agentBehavior: If field and suggestedFix are present, revise the payload and retry only when the user's intent is unchanged.
If the failed operation is create, update, or delete, ask for confirmation again before retrying when the effective write changes.
Use requestId when reporting unresolved API errors.

customDate.description: Required only when period is custom. Omit for monthly or yearly subscriptions.
